signals.title: NZD/CAD — G8 Bilateral Theme + BoC Hawkish Shift
signals.text: NZD/CAD at 0.8163 — NZD leads G8 performance (+0.630%) while CAD lags (-0.647%) despite BoC Governor Macklem's warning of consecutive hikes if oil remains high. The 1.277% G8 spread signals a strong bilateral theme. NZD/USD COT LF net short -16,833 (23% long) vs RBNZ hike bias creates a structural short-squeeze risk that is currently overpowering CAD's oil-linked hawkishness.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Retail Divergence
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7246 — COT LF net long +48,299 (74.8% long) indicates heavy institutional backing, while retail sentiment is at an extreme 89% short. This 150bp institutional-retail divergence suggests retail is aggressively fading the trend; a continuation higher triggers retail capitulation. RBA 4.35% vs Fed 3.75% provides a +60bp carry advantage supporting the move.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical + Carry + Intervention Risk
signals.text: USD/JPY at 156.25 — Fed-BoJ carry of +300bp remains the primary driver as VIX at 17.4 (normal volatility) limits safe-haven JPY demand. However, Treasury Secretary Bessent's upcoming Tokyo meetings and Goolsbee's 'inflationary shock' warnings from the US-Iran conflict introduce headline risk. JPY COT LF net short -88,543 remains crowded; verbal intervention risk rises if price tests 160.00.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Correlation Break + Carry
signals.text: EUR/USD at 1.1759 — Correlation with EuroStoxx has shifted to +0.721 (z=+1.97σ), diverging from the -0.145 norm. EUR is currently trading as a pro-cyclical asset alongside equities rather than its historical inverse relationship. Fed 3.75% vs ECB 2.0% (+175bp USD advantage) structurally limits upside unless the US-Iran conflict further weighs on the DXY.
